mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and eases financial conditions, so equity valuations rise and S&P 500 forward returns are positive over the next 20 trading days when the policy rate is trending down.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward returns are not positive in a majority of months with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if the rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in CPIAUCSL relative to prior months increases inflation shock risk, compresses real returns, and raises the odds of negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days when inflation accelerates beyond a threshold.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises more than 0.4% month over month
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations deliver positive 5-day S&P 500 returns more than 60% of the time, or if the sign of the effect reverses for two consecutive rolling years.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP and retail sales both strengthen together, the market interprets it as durable end-demand improvement that supports earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint GDPC1 and RSAFS improvements do not outperform either series alone in predicting 60-day returns, or if the combined signal has Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals weakening household income and slower sales growth, so S&P 500 forward returns are negative over the next 20 trading days after unemployment crosses above its recent trend.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if post-trigger 20-day returns are not negative on average, or if the trigger no longer has predictive power after controlling for recent market volatility.

mechanism_narrative: Improving INDPRO indicates stronger industrial output and better operating leverage for cyclicals, which lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days when production momentum turns upward.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO momentum fails to coincide with above-median 60-day returns, or if the effect is absent in more than half of rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in PAYEMS and rise in UNRATE identifies a labor-market rollover that should precede weaker earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor signal does not outperform either PAYEMS or UNRATE alone, or if triggered weeks do not show a negative average 5-day return.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in HOUST reflects weakening housing demand and a negative wealth-effect channel that should pressure equities, so S&P 500 returns are negative over the next 20 trading days after housing starts fall below trend.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 3-month moving average by more than 5%
failure_condition: Reject if below-trend HOUST readings do not precede negative 20-day returns, or if the effect is not statistically different from zero in any 2-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a high FEDFUNDS rate combined with rising CPIAUCSL signals restrictive policy that hurts equity duration and compresses multiples, implying negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint tightening-plus-inflation signal does not predict negative 60-day returns, or if the sign weakens materially when FEDFUNDS is above the recent median.
